mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150,000 persons in a single month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below -2.0% signals weakening consumer demand that portends earnings downgrades and inventory corrections, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite sharp retail sales declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth < 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite simultaneous production weakness and unemployment rise, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling robust construction demand and forward economic momentum that supports equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,200 thousand units AND GDPC1 growth > 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong housing and GDP growth, or interaction term adds no explanatory power (R² improvement < 0.01) in multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, signaling monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression and equity risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening lag with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflation-rate policy lag, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% in a single month, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment spikes, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3.0%, signaling healthy supply-side expansion without cost-push pressure that supports profit margins and equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 1.0% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL growth < 3.0% year-over-year
regime_hint: non-inflationary supply expansion or productivity-driven growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong production growth and stable inflation, or interaction term does not improve model fit (R² improvement < 0.01).
